name: Fish, Clams and Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 4 Idaho potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2 inch cubes
6 oz Pork fat, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 large Onion, coarsely chopped (1/4 inch pieces)
2 cups Clam juice, fresh, canned or bottled
1 cup Fresh sweet corn or 16 oz Unsalted canned corn, strained
1 lb Fresh fish, cut into 1-inch square pieces
24 Fresh shelled chowder clams or 1 cup Strained canned clams, chopped up
recipeInstructions:
1. Use an enamel pot that holds about one gallon of water. Add 2-3 ears of sweet corn to boiling water. Cook until almost tender, then cut the corn off the cob.
2. Add the potatoes to the same water and boil them until half done (about 5 minutes), strain, and set aside.
3. On a medium heat, brown the pork fat until it's nearly crisp; remove any fattiest bits.
4. Add the onion and when it's caramelized, add the garlic, celery, and potatoes. Brown each ingredient slightly before adding the next.
5. If needed, add butter to the pot.
6. On a low heat, add 1 cup of clam juice, white wine, corn, and all the spices. Simmer for a few minutes to reduce slightly.
7. Add the remaining clam juice, clams, and fish; simmer another 5 minutes max.
8. Salt lightly with coarse salt if necessary.
9. Stir in the light cream until it starts to simmer; serve hot. Serves 4
10. If making chowder without clams, prepare a fish stock by boiling 1/2 cup of white wine, celery stalk, parsley, bay leaf, thyme, onion, salt, peppercorns, and fish bones/head in cold water for about 20 minutes; strain the liquid into bowls.
